items.headline: U.S. House bill would erect crypto-theft task force across law enforcement agencies
items.subheadline: New legislation from a bipartisan duo would set up a multi-agency group under the U.S. attorney general to take a lead on cryptocurrency theft cases.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Former SEC, CFTC Chair Gary Gensler argues that prediction markets don't overrule state regulations
items.subheadline: The former regulator joined a number of interest groups in arguing that prediction markets are overstepping their bounds by offering sports-related contracts.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: FTX's Sam Bankman-Fried loses appeal of criminal conviction on fraud, conspiracy charges
items.subheadline: The onetime FTX CEO did not persuade a panel of judges that his trial was unfair, the appeals court panel ruled Friday.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
items.subheadline: Pruden argues that to defend against a quantum computer capable of cryptographically relevant operations, we need post-quantum cryptography and regulatory coordination that the industry has been deferring for years.
items.author: Alex  Pruden

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: Kalshi’s crypto perpetuals spark debate over whether they’re futures or swaps
items.subheadline: A clash between derivatives veterans is exposing a deeper fight over how U.S. regulators should classify crypto perpetual contracts.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Bloomberg Analyst: Most Bitcoin ETF Investors Have Stayed Put Despite Outflows
items.subheadline: Bitcoin ETF investors have pulled billions this year, but the broader crypto ETF market remains more resilient than recent headlines suggest.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: U.S. House tax committee weighs crypto bills, including relief for small transactions
items.subheadline: Seven draft bills are being circulated by the House Ways and Means Committee ahead of a hearing next week, including proposals to ease small-gain, mining and staking burdens.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: The agentic CFO in your pocket
items.subheadline: Chalom explains that retail investors have never had the opportunity to access and manage their own digital treasury desk. Until now.
items.author: Joseph Chalom

items.headline: Live BTC price updates: Bitcoin narrows early losses, returns to $62,000 as Nasdaq bounces to close down 1%
items.subheadline: Crypto prices rose on Monday following last week's crash, but the bears still appear to be in control.
items.author: Stephen Alpher

items.headline: Metaplanet acquires Siiibo Securities in $13.1m deal to advance Bitcoin strategy
items.subheadline: The approximately $13.1 million acquisition gives Metaplanet a regulated securities platform to develop Bitcoin-linked investment products.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
items.subheadline: Stand With Crypto UK is telling its members to file formal complaints with high-street banks over sector-wide transfer bans.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Singapore bank DBS to offer tokenized gold to retail customers
items.subheadline: Each token is backed by one gram of physical gold held by DBS in a dedicated vault in Singapore.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Ondo Finance pushes into tokenized investment products, hires former Invesco ETF chief
items.subheadline: John Hoffman will spearhead Ondo's expansion from tokenizing individual assets to full investment portfolios and strategies.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Citi opens new route into private markets with tokenized share offering
items.subheadline: The bank said it is using blockchain technology to connect investors with private company equity through digital depositary receipts.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Tether leads $1.4 billion funding round in German robotics company Neura
items.subheadline: The investment is part of the stablecoin giant’s expansion into industries outside of crypto.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Canton Network developer Digital Asset raises $355 million to bring capital markets onchain
items.subheadline: The investment comes as blockchains designed for big banks and institutions are having a moment, with Stripe's Tempo and Circle's Arc raising hundreds of millions of dollars.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Big banks are ditching private blockchains to build tokenized cash networks on public infrastructure
items.subheadline: A live banking trial in Switzerland alongside UBS and PostFinance is creating the blueprint for unified, bank-native stablecoin infrastructure.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: While BTC price holds near $63,000, some data points to pain ahead for bulls: Crypto Markets Today
items.subheadline: Onchain data shows the bitcoin market price is only just above its realized price and demand is weakening, especially from ETFs.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Monero (XMR) prices rocket 33% to $438 amid $120 million onchain laundering maze
items.subheadline: Onchain investigator ZachXBT traced the funds through Monero, instant swaps and cross-chain bridges, a pattern used to launder money.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP prediction: Sentiment falls to an eight-month low, and that has been a buy signal before
items.subheadline: Santiment's weighted sentiment gauge for XRP just hit its weakest reading since October 2025. The firm notes the token's strongest rebounds have tended to start when the crowd was this checked out.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Botanix bet big on 'Bitcoin DeFi.' Its shutdown suggests users never cared
items.subheadline: The project cited market conditions and a broader indifference among the cryptocurrency industry to establishing greater utility on the Bitcoin network.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Solana (SOL) news: World Series of Poker adds SOL payments for tournament buy-ins
items.subheadline: The move marks an integration of blockchain-based payments into a major live sporting and gaming event.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: developers are exploring new token standards as privacy returns to focus
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at where privacy is headed in the Ethereum ecosystem
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: SpaceX stock is coming to Solana the same day it lists on Nasdaq
items.subheadline: The firms making that possible say eligible shares can also be converted back into tokens, creating a bridge between traditional brokerage accounts and blockchain-based markets.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Coinbase (COIN) news: new AI agent accounts that can trade and spend on your behalf
items.subheadline: "Coinbase for Agents" is a new platform that lets AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude connect to users’ Coinbase accounts to trade crypto, access data and eventually make payments and purchases autonomously.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: LG Electronics is taking advertisements onchain. Arbitrum helped
items.subheadline: LG Electronics has built a blockchain-based advertising platform with the help of Arbitrum.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live BTC prices: SpaceX rally loses momentum, while bitcoin trades near $64,000
items.subheadline: Crypto firmed across the board following the largest equity listing in history. The token most tied to Elon Musk traded right in line with the market.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
items.subheadline: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.
items.author: Robin Singh

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: A quick review of the Ways and Means tax bills: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: The House Ways and Means Committee is gearing up for its big tax push.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Sam Bankman-Fried officially asks Donald Trump for a presidential pardon
items.subheadline: The fallen crypto mogul serving a 25-year sentence filed a clemency petition, betting on Donald Trump's history of crypto pardons even though the president had told him not to count on one.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: U.K.'s FCA moves to allow mutual funds 10% exposure to crypto ETNs
items.subheadline: The FCA suggested allowing UK UCITS schemes and some NURS to invest in crypto ETNs in its latest quarterly consultation paper.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Crypto tax bills a work-in-progress as U.S. House lawmakers pose concerns
items.subheadline: The effort to push several tax bills is meant to be bipartisan, but the parties may not be comfortable with all details of the seven bills weighed by the panel.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Prediction markets get first U.S. rule proposal as CFTC pursues contract reviews
items.subheadline: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission opened a proposed rule for public comment that sets an approach to determine contracts in the "public interest."
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Philippines' central bank complicates Binance's return to the country
items.subheadline: Binance and its local partner do not hold the necessary license required to operate in the country, the Philippine central bank said, according to local media reports.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Japan’s parliament poised to pass sweeping bill to regulate crypto like stocks
items.subheadline: The new rules, which are expected to come into effect in 2027, aim to foster innovation and crypto market growth to meet internal and external demand for digital asset services.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: SpaceX opens at $162 in blockbuster Nasdaq debut
items.subheadline: Shares were initially priced at $135 a share during the Thursday IPO.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: VanEck bets BNB’s real-world usage can stand out in a crowded crypto ETF market
items.subheadline: VanEck says BNB’s user activity and revenue generation make it a stronger long-term crypto investment case than many blockchain projects still selling a vision.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Bitcoin hit bottom at $59,000 marking end to the crypto winter, says Standard Chartered analyst
items.subheadline: Senior market analyst Geoffrey Kendrick pointed to the SpaceX IPO and a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal as the dual catalysts ending the recent crypto sell-off.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: The SpaceX IPO scramble brings early lesson for tokenized stocks
items.subheadline: Crypto platforms promised early access to the blockbuster SpaceX IPO through tokenized shares. The problem wasn't technology, but getting the actual stock.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: BTC, ETH, SOL price news: Bitcoin’s worst week in months got a late macro rescue
items.subheadline: A tiny Strategy sale raised a bigger question about Saylor’s never-sell stance, while easing Iran fears, and a strong SpaceX debut helped risk assets recover.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Anthropic's pre-IPO shares fall as US government shuts down Fable, Mythos models
items.subheadline: A jailbreak report prompted Washington to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 from all users. Anthropic complied but pushed back hard, saying the finding sets a standard that would halt the entire AI industry.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: What happens to Satoshi's BTC when Bitcoin's quantum problem is fixed?
items.subheadline: A Coinbase-convened panel says Bitcoin should start preparing for quantum attacks now, but declines to take a position on whether millions of vulnerable coins, including many linked to Satoshi Nakamoto, should eventually be frozen.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ripple wants AI agents to pay in XRP and RLUSD. The market is still mostly USDC
items.subheadline: The XRPL AI Starter Kit gives developers tools for agent payments, but early x402 activity has clustered on Base and Solana. Ripple’s bet is that XRPL’s speed, low fees and RLUSD can win a piece of that flow.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Why crypto's future may look more like traditional markets
items.subheadline: As digital assets mature the industry should borrow more from traditional market infrastructure, especially credit, clearing and collateral systems, Mercer argues.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Saylor to Musk: Thanks to you, 25% of 'Mag8' firms now hold bitcoin
items.subheadline: Strategy chairman Michael Saylor congratulated Elon Musk on SpaceX's historic Nasdaq debut Friday, coining the term "Mag8" and noting that 25% of the group now holds bitcoin on the balance sheet.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Perpetual futures could become crypto's next ETF moment
items.subheadline: John Palmer, head of derivatives at Kraken, said he expects sophisticated traders to lead adoption of newly approved U.S. perpetual futures, with broader institutional participation likely to follow over time.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Tokenization mirrors the $20 trillion ETF boom as blockchain and AI converge, new Ondo exec says
items.subheadline: Tokenization is laying the groundwork for autonomous investing and real-time portfolio management, Ondo's new head of portfolio products John Hoffman said.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Wall Street is moving past crypto pilots and deeper into Ethereum, says Etherealize founder
items.subheadline: In an interview with CoinDesk, Etherealize cofounder Vivek Raman said Ethereum is currently in a transitional phase where the infrastructure has largely been built, but the scale of adoption has yet to be fully reflected in ETH itself.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Bitcoin surpasses $64,00 as Friday's ETF inflows reach highest level since May 14
items.subheadline: Bitcoin traded above $64,000 on Saturday, supported by its strongest ETF inflows in a month and growing optimism around geopolitical developments.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Here's what SpaceX's IPO means for its 18,000 bitcoin (BTC) holdings
items.subheadline: The largest company on public markets now holds bitcoin as a treasury reserve, not as a business model. Its first earnings cycles will test which version of corporate crypto survives a bear market.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Here's what Claude Fable 5 means for crypto and DeFi
items.subheadline: Anthropic’s new Claude Fable 5 puts powerful cyber tools behind safety filters. DeFi, already hit by more than $840 million in hacks this year, is one of the industries with the most to lose if the filters fail.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
